svg.text: বাংলা · 1,000 years · Indo-Aryan · abugida · left to right · 11 vowels · 39 consonants
— · — BENGALI · THE LANGUAGE OF GOLDEN BENGAL — · —
Bengali (বাংলা Bangla) is the 7th most spoken language · 300 million speakers worldwide
official language of Bangladesh and West Bengal · also used in Assam and Tripura
abugida: every consonant carries inherent vowel /ɔ/ · diacritics override it
script evolved from Brahmi via Siddhamatrika · Unicode block U+0980–U+09FF
vowels stand alone at syllable start · as diacritics on consonants otherwise · /ɔ/ is the inherent vowel
— · — VOWEL DIACRITICS · কার · KAAR · SHOWN ON CONSONANT ক (K) — · —
consonant + diacritic replaces /ɔ/ · hasanta ্ removes inherent vowel entirely · ক alone = /kɔ/
ক্ = /k/ (hasanta kills /ɔ/) · conjunct: ক + ্ + ষ = ক্ষ /kʃ/ · clusters of up to 4 consonants fuse into one ligature
ordered by place of articulation: velar → palatal → retroflex → dental → labial → other
hasanta ্ (U+09CD) joins consonants · removes inherent vowel · creates ligature clusters of up to 4
ড় /ṝ/ retroflex flap · ঢ় /ṝh/ · য় /ẏ/ · ৎ final /t/ · ং /ŋ/ anusvara · ঃ visarga · ঁ chandrabindu (nasal)

THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
